title: Lab 48: SSH Key Authentication And SCP
description: Use a key pair and scp between the two lab hosts.

tasks: Ensure user bridge48 exists on both clientvm and servervm, and set the password on both systems to cinder9.
As user bridge48 on clientvm, generate an ED25519 SSH key pair with no passphrase.
Configure passwordless SSH access for bridge48 from clientvm to servervm using the public key.
Using scp over SSH, copy /home/bridge48/payload.txt from clientvm to /home/bridge48/inbox/ on servervm.
hints: This lab does need a home directory because the key pair lives under ~/.ssh.
ssh-copy-id is the quickest way to seed authorized_keys.
checks: getent passwd bridge48 >/dev/null && getent shadow bridge48 | awk -F: 'length($2)>0 && $2 !~ /^(!!?|\*|LK|NP)$/' >/dev/null && ssh admin@servervm sudo getent passwd bridge48 >/dev/null && ssh admin@servervm sudo getent shadow bridge48 | awk -F: 'length($2)>0 && $2 !~ /^(!!?|\*|LK|NP)$/' >/dev/null
solution_outline: Create bridge48 on both systems and set the password.
Generate a no-passphrase ED25519 key pair on clientvm.
Install the public key on servervm and verify passwordless SSH.
Copy the payload file into the remote inbox directory.
task_titles: Ensure bridge48 exists on both systems
Enable passwordless SSH from clientvm to servervm
